userStories.title: Seed: importar catalogo do Data Dragon (idempotente)
userStories.description: As a curator, I want the champion/item/trait/augment catalog imported so that comps and the builder use real game data.
acceptanceCriteria: src/server/ddragon.ts busca dados do set atual no Data Dragon
prisma/seed.ts faz upsert por apiId+set em Champion/Item/Trait/Augment e popula ChampionTrait
Trait.breakpoints e Champion.cost preenchidos (necessarios ao motor de sinergias e ao builder)

Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Catalog seed implemented. src/server/ddragon.ts getCatalog() fetches the full TFT export from Community Dragon (raw.communitydragon.org/latest/cdragon/tft/en_us.json) — NOT classic ddragon, which lacks trait breakpoints AND champion<->trait links (both AC-required for the synergy engine/builder). Current set resolved dynamically as highest mainline mutator TFTSet<number> -> 'TFTSet17'. Normalizes champions (apiId/name/cost/iconUrl/traitNames, filtered to playable cost 1-7 with traits), traits (breakpoints = sorted distinct minUnits), items (classified into ItemType via apiName keyword + composition, junk/MarketOffering/nameless filtered), augments (set-scoped). .tex asset paths rewritten to CDN PNG via assetUrl(); champion icon HEAD-verified HTTP 200 image/png. prisma/seed.ts upserts all four catalog models on @@unique([apiId,set]) and populates ChampionTrait. Runner: tsx via package.json prisma.seed; run with `npm run db:seed` (NOT `prisma db seed` directly — tsx not on PATH). Seeded set TFTSet17: 63 champions, 44 traits, 360 items, 53 augments, 133 championTraits (all counts >0). IDEMPOTENT — proven by seeding twice: counts identical (133/133). GOTCHA fixed: Set 17 has 8 traits all named 'Stargazer'; the original name->id map was non-deterministic under concurrent batch writes and drifted 133->139 on re-seed. Fix: pin each trait name to one canonical apiId (shortest apiId, then lexicographic = base trait) and look up by apiId. Champion.cost (0 missing) and Trait.breakpoints filled (42/44; 'Choose Trait'/'God-Blessed' meta-traits legitimately []). typecheck + lint clean. Backend-only — no browser verification in AC. Added tsx devDep + prisma/CLAUDE.md + src/server/CLAUDE.md.

userStories.title: Seed: patch atual, SiteConfig e comps de exemplo
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a current patch and sample published comps so that the public UI has data to render and verify.

Seed cria ao menos 3 comps PUBLISHED em tiers diferentes, com traits, units (early/core/flex), carry+itens, item priority, augments+augmentPriority, posicoes de board e guia preenchido (whenToPlay + earlyGame/midGame/lateGame)
Ao menos uma comp marcada como introduzida no patch atual (badge Novo) e uma com patchUpdated no patch atual
npx prisma db seed completa sem erro
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extended prisma/seed.ts: seedCatalog() (US-009, returns {set,setNumber}) now runs first, then seedSampleData(set,setNumber). Creates/updates previous patch (version `${setNumber}.1`=17.1, releasedAt 2026-06-01) + current patch (`${setNumber}.2`=17.2, releasedAt 2026-06-15), both upserted on version; SiteConfig upserted on id=1 with currentSet=TFTSet17 + currentPatchId=current. Builds 3 PUBLISHED comps in distinct tiers (S/A/B) referencing REAL seeded catalog rows queried back from the DB (champions by apiId, items type=COMPLETED, augments) — no hardcoded apiIds, follows whatever set the catalog loaded. Each comp: 12-champion disjoint window sorted by cost -> 3 EARLY (off-board), 7 CORE (on-board hexes), 2 FLEX; the 2 most expensive CORE are carries (isCarry, starLevel 3, 3 items each = 6 carryItems); 4 CompTrait (level=unit-count per trait), 4-item CompItemPriority, 3 CompAugment, augmentPriority array (rotated per comp), board positions, full guide (whenToPlay + earlyGame/midGame/lateGame + tips). Badges: comp #1 (n-o-v-a-blitzcrank, S) introduced in current patch => NOVO; comp #2 (meeple-jhin, A) introduced in previous + patchUpdated=current => ATUALIZADO; comp #3 (dark-star-master-yi, B) introduced in previous, no update => none. IDEMPOTENT: comps lack a natural key so each is deleteMany({where:{slug}}) (cascades children) then nested db.comp.create; slugs deduped within a run via uniqueSlug. Verified by seeding TWICE: identical (3 comps, same slugs, no drift). releasedAt/publishedAt use fixed ISO dates so re-seeds are stable. `npm run db:seed` completes with no error; typecheck + lint clean. Backend-only story — no browser verification in AC. Updated prisma/CLAUDE.md with the US-010 seed conventions.

userStories.notes: Added src/server/services/badges.ts exporting getCompBadges(comp, currentPatchId) -> { isNew, isUpdated }. isNew = comp.patchIntroducedId === currentPatchId; isUpdated = comp.patchUpdatedId === currentPatchId && !isNew (Novo precedence). Accepts a minimal BadgeComp interface (patchIntroducedId: string, patchUpdatedId: string | null) so a full Prisma Comp satisfies it structurally. GUARD: when currentPatchId is null/undefined (SiteConfig.currentPatchId is nullable) returns both-false early — also avoids a null===null false-positive on isUpdated when a comp has no patchUpdatedId. TEST RUNNER (first in repo): Node's built-in test runner via tsx — package.json `test` script = `tsx --test "src/**/*.test.ts"` (quotes matter: Node 24 expands the glob, not the shell). Tests use node:test + node:assert/strict, co-located as *.test.ts. src/server/services/badges.test.ts covers all AC cases: nova, atualizada, nenhuma (both no-update variants), atualizada+nova (Novo precedence), plus null/undefined currentPatchId. Verified: `npm test` -> 7 pass / 0 fail; `npm run typecheck` exit 0; `npm run lint` no warnings/errors. Backend-only story — no browser verification in AC.

userStories.notes: Added public read queries under src/server/queries/. THREE modules: config.ts (getSiteConfig() -> SiteConfig+currentPatch, getCurrentSet()), tierlist.ts (getPublishedComps(set?), groupCompsByTier, TIER_ORDER), comp.ts (getCompBySlug(slug)). All use the { db } PrismaClient singleton. getPublishedComps(set?) defaults set to SiteConfig.currentSet (returns [] if unconfigured), filters status:PUBLISHED, orders by tier(S,A,B,C,X via enum decl order),publishedAt desc,name; returns lean CompCard rows (main traits ordered + carries only via units where isCarry, each with champion id/name/iconUrl/cost) — comp scalars incl patchIntroducedId/patchUpdatedId come free so cards feed getCompBadges directly. groupCompsByTier(comps) buckets into all 5 fixed bands. getCompBySlug(slug) uses findFirst({slug,status:PUBLISHED}) (NOT findUnique — needs the status filter) and returns null for missing/draft/archived so US-017 can 404; CompDetail includes full tree: patchIntroduced/patchUpdated, traits(trait), units(champion, items(item)) all ordered by order, itemPriority(item), augments(augment); board pos = boardRow/boardCol scalars. userStories.notes: Added src/components/comp-card.tsx (presentational server component). Props: comp (CompCard row from queries/tierlist) + currentPatchId. ICONS use next/image (avoids the @next/next/no-img-element lint WARNING that plain triggers — that warning would fail the 'No warnings' gate); carry icon = fill in a relative 40px box, trait icon = 14x14. userStories.notes: Board positioning added to the shared comp-detail page. NEW src/components/comp-board.tsx exports <CompBoard units={comp.units}/>, rendered below <CompItemPriority/> (above <CompUnits/>) inside the existing `mt-8 flex flex-col gap-8` wrapper in src/app/(public)/comps/[slug]/page.tsx. It is a CLIENT component ('use client') because of the 'Mostrar nomes' toggle (useState) — this does NOT make the page dynamic (no request-time APIs), so the comp route stays 'Static route' (verified). Renders a 4-row × 7-col honeycomb: for each hex it looks up byHex.get(`${row}-${col}`) from units with non-null boardRow/boardCol (0-indexed; off-board EARLY/FLEX with null coords excluded); returns null if no unit is on-board. Layout is percentage-based so it scales to any width with NO horizontal scroll: each cell is w-[13.333%] (=100/7.5) shrink-0 aspect-square, odd rows get pl-[6.667%] (half a hex) for the offset, and rows use marginTop:-2.5% to nest. Hexes use a pointy-top clip-path polygon (50% 0,100% 25%,100% 75%,50% 100%,0% 75%,0% 25%) applied to layered divs (tile-background layer inset-[2px] + image layer inset-[3px], both clipped) so the ring shows as a hex border around a next/image fill icon (raw.communitydragon.org already whitelisted). Empty hexes = bg-muted/30 tiles (form the grid backdrop). CARRY HIGHLIGHT (isCarry): an extra inset-0 bg-brand-gradient glow hex behind + bg-brand-gradient ring + thicker inset-[4px] image inset, so carries show a prominent purple→cyan gradient ring vs the slate-600 ring on other units. 'Mostrar nomes' button (aria-pressed) toggles a name label overlaid at each hex bottom (text-shadow for legibility); label reads 'Ocultar nomes' when on. Board wrapper is mx-auto w-full max-w-md (448px desktop, full width on mobile) in a rounded bordered bg-card/40 panel. Quality: typecheck exit 0, lint 'No ESLint warnings or errors', 7/7 tests pass. Browser (Playwright, dev server :3001 — a stale prior server holds :3000): /comps/n-o-v-a-blitzcrank DESKTOP 1280x720 — 'Posicionamento' region with 7 CORE units at correct hexes (row0 Bel'Veth/Aurora/Diana, row1 Aurelion Sol/Corki, row3 Bard/Blitzcrank — matches seed CORE_HEXES), 7 board icons loaded (0 broken, naturalWidth 64), exactly 2 gradient (carry) hexes (Bard+Blitzcrank), board inner width 448px, page 'Static route', no h-scroll (1280==1280); clicking 'Mostrar nomes' → button becomes 'Ocultar nomes' (aria-pressed true) and all 7 names overlay their hexes. userStories.notes: SEO layer added. NEW src/lib/site.ts (client-safe, no server imports): SITE_URL (from NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL, default https://metacomps.gg, trailing slash stripped), SITE_NAME, SITE_DESCRIPTION, SITE_LOCALE (pt_BR), absoluteUrl(path). Root src/app/layout.tsx metadata now sets metadataBase=new URL(SITE_URL) (so all relative canonical/OG URLs resolve absolute), a title template `%s · MetaComps`, applicationName, and site-wide openGraph(type website)+twitter(summary_large_image) defaults that pages inherit. generateMetadata on /comps/[slug] (page.tsx): title `${name} — ${tierLabel}` (template appends brand), description = comp.whenToPlay (clamped to 160 chars w/ ellipsis) else a synthesized 'Comp de TFT <tier> no patch <v>. Carries: … Sinergias: …', alternates.canonical `/comps/<slug>`, openGraph(type article, url, publishedTime/modifiedTime, title carrying tier+set+patch) + matching twitter card; when comp is null returns { robots:{index:false,follow:false} }. Homepage (public)/page.tsx got a static `metadata = { alternates:{canonical:'/'} }`. app/sitemap.ts (revalidate 3600) lists the homepage + one entry per PUBLISHED comp (new query getPublishedCompsForSitemap() -> {slug,updatedAt}, status:PUBLISHED only, lastModified from updatedAt); app/robots.ts allows all, disallows /admin, points at absoluteUrl('/sitemap.xml') + host. opengraph-image.tsx (1200x630, next/og ImageResponse, flexbox-only inline styles) renders the comp name + a tier badge in the tier color (hex mirrors the globals.css tier tokens) + set/patch + up-to-2 carries; twitter-image.tsx re-exports it so twitter:image is populated too. JSON-LD Article <script> on the comp page (headline, description, datePublished/dateModified, author/publisher=Organization MetaComps, mainEntityOfPage). GOTCHA fixed during runtime verify: getCompData reads through unstable_cache, which JSON-serializes results, so Date fields (publishedAt/updatedAt) come back as STRINGS on cache HITS (real Dates only on the first cache-miss) — calling .toISOString() directly 500s on the 2nd request; added toIso(v)=new Date(v).toISOString() which handles both. Quality: `rm -rf .next` then typecheck exit 0, lint 'No ESLint warnings or errors', 7/7 tests pass. Runtime-verified against the live seeded DB (dev :3000): /robots.txt (Allow /, Disallow /admin, Host+Sitemap=metacomps.gg), /sitemap.xml (homepage + exactly the 3 PUBLISHED comps, no drafts, lastmod present), /comps/n-o-v-a-blitzcrank HTML has <title> w/ template, <link canonical>, meta description, og:title/description/url/type=article/image(1200x630)/image:alt, twitter:card=summary_large_image + twitter:title/description/image, and the Article JSON-LD; og-image + twitter-image both return HTTP 200 image/png, valid PNG magic, 1200x630, non-blank. .env.example documents NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL. userStories.description: As a developer, I want a synergy engine so that the builder can show active traits from placed units.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/synergy.ts: funcao pura recebe unidades posicionadas + breakpoints do catalogo e retorna traits ativos com nivel

userStories.notes: Synergy engine added as a PURE, client-safe module src/lib/synergy.ts (NO imports — runs in server components, the 'use client' builder US-025/026, and the Node test runner alike; sits alongside src/lib/tiers.ts). computeSynergies(units, traits) -> ActiveTrait[]. Inputs: SynergyUnit { championId, traits: string[] } (positioned units — board position is irrelevant to synergy counting so it's omitted) + TraitInfo { key, name, breakpoints: number[] } (the catalog breakpoints). KEY-AGNOSTIC by design: SynergyUnit.traits and TraitInfo.key just need the SAME string key — the catalog references traits by NAME (CatalogChampion.traitNames) while the DB references them by id (ChampionTrait), so the caller picks one and the engine works with either (decoupled from the data source, like the badges service). UNIQUE COUNTING (AC 'nao duplica a mesma unidade'): exported helper countUnitsByTrait(units) dedupes by championId (a duplicate copy of a champion on a 2nd hex adds nothing — skipped) AND collapses a trait repeated on one champion via new Set(unit.traits); both guarded by tests. BREAKPOINT RESOLUTION: normalizeBreakpoints sorts asc + de-dupes + drops non-positive defensively; a trait is active once count >= breakpoints[0]; tier = 1-based index of the highest breakpoint <= count; ActiveTrait carries count (the displayed number e.g. 'Mecha 4'), activeBreakpoint (threshold reached), tier (style tier 1/2/3), nextBreakpoint (null when maxed), maxed. Traits with [] breakpoints (meta-traits like 'Choose Trait') never activate; traits on units but absent from the catalog are ignored; catalog traits with 0 units are skipped. Output sorted for a synergy panel: tier desc, count desc, name asc. Tests: src/lib/synergy.test.ts (17 cases) cover empty board, below-first-breakpoint inactive, activation at tier 1, mid-tier resolution + nextBreakpoint, exactly-on-threshold tier advance, maxed (no next), count above top, single-breakpoint trait, duplicate champion counts once (+ two copies alone don't activate), repeated-trait-on-one-champion, multi-trait champion contributing to all traits, empty-breakpoints never active, unknown trait ignored, full sort order (tier>count>name), name tiebreak, and breakpoint normalization. Verified: npm test -> 24 pass / 0 fail (17 synergy + 7 badges); npm run typecheck exit 0; npm run lint 'No ESLint warnings or errors'. Backend/pure-function story — no browser verification in AC. US-026 SynergyPanel consumes computeSynergies; the builder feeds it placed champions (with catalog traitNames) + catalog trait breakpoints.

Login form src/components/admin/login-form.tsx ('use client') uses signIn('credentials',{redirect:false}) then router.push(callbackUrl)+refresh, shows inline 'E-mail ou senha inválidos.' on failure; LogoutButton uses signOut({callbackUrl:'/admin/login'}). /admin/login (src/app/admin/login/page.tsx) is public (reads callbackUrl+error from searchParams server-side, sanitizes callbackUrl to /admin-prefixed only) and /admin (src/app/admin/page.tsx) is the minimal protected landing (requireRole -> shows email+role+Sair; US-031 replaces with the dashboard). TYPES: src/types/next-auth.d.ts augments Session (user.id+role) via 'next-auth' AND JWT via '@auth/core/jwt' — GOTCHA: augmenting 'next-auth/jwt' does NOT work because it's a pure `export * from "@auth/core/jwt"` re-export, so the callback's token (typed via @auth/core/jwt.JWT) never sees it; augment @auth/core/jwt directly (symptom was `if(token.id)` narrowing unknown->{} => TS2322). ENV: AUTH_SECRET (openssl rand -base64 32), AUTH_TRUST_HOST=true (non-Vercel dev), ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_PASSWORD documented in .env.example; local .env (gitignored) has real values. SEED: prisma/seed.ts seedAdminUser() upserts the ADMIN curator (bcryptjs hash of ADMIN_PASSWORD, keyed on unique email, idempotent). Deps added: next-auth@beta, bcryptjs, @types/bcryptjs.
